question: To examine the relationship between male-pattern baldness and myocardial infarction, investigators studied 655 men admitted to a hospital for a first nonfatal myocardial infarction (cases) and 772 admitted to the same hospitals with noncardiac diagnoses (controls). This is a casecontrol sample because the investigator selected fixed numbers of subjects based on the status of the response variable (myocardial infarction). The extent of baldness was assessed in subjects using several methods.
